audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in dntest.c are guarded: argv[1] access is protected by the argc<2 check (L68), ldap_dn2str(NULL,...) is safe because ldap_dn2bv_x explicitly handles NULL dn at getdn.c:3025, and ldap_dcedn2dn(NULL) is safe because ldap_dn_normalize returns early on NULL input (getdn.c:371-373). Additionally, dntest.c is a standalone CLI developer test utility (listed under PROGRAMS in libldap/Makefile.in), not part of the slapd server daemon — no external LDAP attacker can reach this code, making the 'external-reachable' reachability classification incorrect.
